name: onResourceStart
description: Fired when a resource starts. The resourceName parameter is the name of the started resource.

name: onResourceStop
description: Fired when a resource stops. Use this to clean up entities, blips, and NUI focus.

name: onResourceStarting
description: Fired before a resource starts. Can be cancelled with CancelEvent().

name: onServerResourceStart
description: Fired on the server when any resource starts.

name: onServerResourceStop
description: Fired on the server when any resource stops.

name: playerConnecting
description: Fired when a player is connecting. Use deferrals to show loading messages or reject the connection.

name: playerDropped
description: Fired when a player disconnects. Source is the player who dropped.

name: playerJoining
description: Fired when a player is joining (after connecting, before fully loaded).

name: onClientMapStart
description: Fired on the client when the game map has loaded.

name: onClientResourceStart
description: Fired on the client when a resource starts.

name: onClientResourceStop
description: Fired on the client when a resource stops.

name: gameEventTriggered
description: Fired when a game event is triggered (e.g. CEventNetworkEntityDamage).

name: baseevents:onPlayerDied
description: Fired when the local player dies (requires baseevents resource).

name: baseevents:onPlayerKilled
description: Fired when the local player is killed by another player (requires baseevents resource).

name: baseevents:enteringVehicle
description: Fired when the player starts entering a vehicle (requires baseevents resource).

name: baseevents:enteredVehicle
description: Fired when the player finishes entering a vehicle (requires baseevents resource).

name: baseevents:leftVehicle
description: Fired when the player exits a vehicle (requires baseevents resource).

name: chatMessage
description: Fired when a chat message is sent. Can be cancelled to suppress the message.

name: rconCommand
description: Fired when an RCON command is executed.

name: explosionEvent
description: Fired when an explosion event is received. Can be cancelled for anti-cheat purposes.
